metadata.name: n8n-node-inventory
metadata.description: Inventario estructurado de nodos y capacidades de n8n para apoyar la skill n8n-workflow-engineering con criterio node-first.

verification_policy.verified-instance: Confirmado en la instancia real de n8n usada por Operaciones.
verification_policy.verified-docs: Confirmado contra documentación oficial de la versión correspondiente.
verification_policy.known-general: Conocimiento general del ecosistema n8n, pendiente de verificar en la instancia real.
verification_policy.candidate: Nodo o capacidad plausible que debe buscarse en la instancia antes de diseñar.
verification_policy.verified-design: Capacidad validada a nivel de diseño interno o arquitectura, aunque no necesariamente como nodo nativo.
verification_policy.deprecated-or-unknown: Nodo dudoso, renombrado, desactualizado o no confirmado.

metadata.node_first_rule: Before using Code or HTTP Request, check whether a native n8n node, AI node, database node, integration node, or visual pattern can solve the need more safely and maintainably.

nodes.name: Manual Trigger
nodes.recommended_use: Manual testing and controlled workflow execution.
prefer_over: Webhook for local/manual tests
Schedule Trigger during design

nodes.name: Schedule Trigger / Cron
nodes.recommended_use: Recurring executions such as syncs, reports, ingestions and scheduled maintenance.
prefer_over: External cron when workflow-native scheduling is enough
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use external scheduler when corporate orchestration, retry policy or deployment constraints require it.

nodes.name: Webhook
nodes.recommended_use: Receive external calls, callbacks, MCP/tool invocations or internal API requests.
prefer_over: Polling when event-driven execution is possible
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use dedicated API service when authentication, rate limiting or complex routing exceeds n8n needs.

nodes.name: Respond to Webhook
nodes.recommended_use: Return controlled responses from webhook-based workflows.
prefer_over: Implicit webhook response for structured APIs
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use a dedicated backend if the response contract is complex or needs advanced auth/session logic.

nodes.name: IF
nodes.recommended_use: Boolean branching based on existence, status, flags or simple comparisons.
prefer_over: Code for simple conditions
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use Code when the condition is multi-step, computed across arrays, or not maintainable in expressions.

nodes.name: Switch
nodes.recommended_use: Route items by source_type, action, category, status or intent.
prefer_over: Multiple nested IF nodes
Code for simple routing
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use Code when routing rules are dynamically generated or require complex scoring.

nodes.name: Merge
nodes.recommended_use: Combine branches, join data, or continue after parallel paths.
prefer_over: Code for simple branch recombination
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use Code when merge logic requires custom matching, conflict resolution or nested reconciliation.

nodes.name: Split in Batches / Loop Over Items
nodes.recommended_use: Process collections safely by batch to control API limits, memory and retries.
prefer_over: Large all-at-once API calls
Code loops for simple iteration
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use Code or external worker when stateful/retry logic is too complex for visual nodes.

nodes.name: Edit Fields / Set
nodes.recommended_use: Create, select, rename or normalize simple fields in visible form.
prefer_over: Code for simple field mapping
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use Code for dynamic schemas, recursive structures, hashing, chunking or advanced validation.

nodes.name: Filter
nodes.recommended_use: Remove unwanted items before expensive or risky steps.
prefer_over: Code for simple filters
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use Code when filtering needs tokenization, similarity, multi-array logic or complex predicates.

nodes.name: Aggregate
nodes.recommended_use: Group, summarize or consolidate multiple items into arrays or summaries.
prefer_over: Code for basic aggregation
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use Code for complex nested groupings, custom reducers or deterministic chunk grouping.

nodes.name: Code
nodes.recommended_use: Deterministic JavaScript for chunking, hashing, deduplication, robust parsing, multi-field validation and complex payload construction.
prefer_over: Visual nodes only when the visual solution would be fragile or unreadable
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use external service when dependencies, runtime limits, CPU/memory or security constraints exceed n8n Code node.

nodes.name: HTTP Request
nodes.recommended_use: Call REST/RPC APIs, MCP endpoints, custom services or unsupported operations.
prefer_over: Native nodes only when they lack the required operation or request control
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Create a dedicated MCP/API wrapper when authentication, redaction, allowlists or reuse are needed.

nodes.name: PostgreSQL
nodes.recommended_use: Query PostgreSQL-compatible databases, including Supabase self-hosted when direct DB access is allowed.
prefer_over: HTTP Request for SQL operations when DB credentials and policies allow direct access
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use Supabase REST/RPC via HTTP Request for API-only access, RLS-aware flows, or custom RPC functions.

nodes.name: Supabase
nodes.recommended_use: Use Supabase operations when the installed node supports required table insert/update/upsert/query behavior.
prefer_over: HTTP Request for simple supported Supabase table operations
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use PostgreSQL node or HTTP Request to REST/RPC endpoints for pgvector, custom match functions, or unsupported upserts.

nodes.name: OpenAI
nodes.recommended_use: LLM generation, extraction, classification or embeddings if supported by the installed node and model list.
prefer_over: HTTP Request for supported OpenAI operations
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use HTTP Request when the node does not support the endpoint, model, response format or payload required.

nodes.name: Embeddings OpenAI
nodes.recommended_use: Generate semantic vectors for RAG pipelines.
prefer_over: HTTP Request for embeddings when the node supports the selected model and dimensions
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use HTTP Request to embeddings endpoint if model/dimensions are unsupported by installed node.

nodes.name: Vector Store / Supabase Vector Store
nodes.recommended_use: Store/search embeddings for RAG if available in the installed AI node set.
prefer_over: Manual HTTP/SQL vector logic when native vector store covers the schema and operation
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use PostgreSQL SQL or Supabase RPC for custom pgvector functions, metadata filters, or strict schema control.

nodes.name: GitLab
nodes.recommended_use: Interact with GitLab repositories if installed node supports needed operations.
prefer_over: HTTP Request for simple supported GitLab operations
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use GitLab Knowledge MCP/API via HTTP Request when read-only controls, secret redaction, file allowlists or custom repo traversal are required.

nodes.name: GitLab Knowledge MCP via HTTP Request
nodes.recommended_use: Read controlled GitLab knowledge files with read-only behavior, authentication, secret redaction and stable tool interface.
prefer_over: Raw GitLab node/API when corporate read-only policy and response shaping are required
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use native GitLab node only for operations that are safely covered and do not need MCP-level controls.

nodes.name: Spreadsheet File
nodes.recommended_use: Read or write CSV/XLSX-style tabular files in ETL/reporting workflows.
prefer_over: Code for standard CSV/XLSX parsing
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use Code/Python/external processing when files are huge, malformed, or require advanced transformations.

nodes.name: Microsoft Teams
nodes.recommended_use: Send internal notifications, alerts or reporting to Teams channels/chats when approved.
prefer_over: HTTP Request to Graph API for common supported operations
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use Microsoft Graph via HTTP Request or official connector when scopes/operations are unsupported.

nodes.name: Microsoft Outlook
nodes.recommended_use: Send/read corporate email when credentials and scopes are approved.
prefer_over: Generic SMTP/HTTP for supported Microsoft mail operations
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use Microsoft Graph via HTTP Request if the native node lacks the operation.

patterns.name: GitLab Knowledge to Supabase pgvector
patterns.description: Ingest curated agent knowledge from GitLab Knowledge into Supabase pgvector using n8n.
recommended_flow: Code for chunking, hashing and deduplication
OpenAI or Embeddings node for vector generation
Supabase, PostgreSQL or HTTP Request for upsert
PostgreSQL or HTTP Request RPC for vector search validation

node_first_decisions.first_choice: GitLab native node if operation is supported and safe
node_first_decisions.fallback: GitLab Knowledge MCP via HTTP Request
External processing service if Code node limits are hit

security_notes: Do not store secrets in workflow notes, Code nodes or Git.
Start with a small subset before full ingestion.
Keep source_path, source_ref, content_hash and chunk_index for traceability.
Prefer idempotent upsert over blind insert.

pending_work: Obtain real n8n version used by Operations.
Export/list installed node types from the real instance.
Confirm whether Supabase, GitLab, OpenAI embeddings and vector store nodes are installed.
Replace candidate/known-general statuses with verified-instance where possible.
Add operation-level schemas for critical nodes.
Add internal workflow examples once validated.
